title: Cell dissociation from nasal and bronchial brushings with cold-active protease for single-cell RNA-seq
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f4igj","text":"This protocol provides details on the cell dissociation that should be performed to obtain single-cell suspensions from nasal epithelium brushings.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ds2mp","text":"Cell dissociation is performed at 4°C to avoid gene expression alterations and maximize viability. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f8hu2","text":"The typical cell number recovery is 200 000 - 300 000 for one brushing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ffnb","text":"Cell suspensions are suitable for single-cell RNA-sequencing protocols.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dafbd","text":"Storage Conditions of Reagents ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":31}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"o60u","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4gsob","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":8,"length":7},{"style":"bold","offset":28,"length":18},{"style":"italic","offset":265,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"e5tud","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2gb81","text":" Required Equipment ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":2,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"flana","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":8,"length":9},{"style":"bold","offset":33,"length":8},{"style":"bold","offset":57,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"co548","text":" The protocol workflow is as follows:  1. Perform brushing of the epithelium of the nasal cavity 2. Dissociation: triturate on ice or store on ice without trituration 3. Remove red blood cells if necessary 4. Prepare cells for Chromium/DropSeq All steps should be performed on ice or at 4°C ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":7,"length":37},{"style":"bold","offset":268,"length":46},{"style":"italic","offset":268,"length":46}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aeord","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"72ks0","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fauhm","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","5_0":"","5_1":"","6_0":"","6_1":"","7_0":"","7_1":""},"colWidths":[50,50],"data":[["Reagent ","Storage Condition  "],["HBSS","4°C"],["Hypothermosol ","4°C "],["20 mM EDTA ","room temperature "],["BSA (Sigma, A8806)","4°C"],["Protease from Bacillus Licheniformis (Sigma, P5380) ","Store 100 μL aliquots (100 mg/mL) in DPBS at -80°C "],["Hoechst 33342 (10 mg/mL) ","4°C "],["NucGreen™ Dead 488 ReadyProbes™ Reagent ","room temperature "]],"guid":"064FBD70260211E98D90DD6675B47F91","legend":"","mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23],"show_headers":true}},"1":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","0_2":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","1_2":""},"colWidths":[50,50,50],"data":[["Equipment ","Supplier ","Catalog no. steps: Perform nasal brush biopsy in the nasal cavity in the inferior nasal conca zone (UBERON_0005922) (to be performed by a medical doctor) (Medi-Globe, GCB-02-18-120, 2.0 mm cytology brush)

Cut the cytology brush and place it in a 5 mL eppendorf tube containing 1 mL of ice-cold dissociation buffer. undetermined PREPARATION OF DISSOCIATION MIX (Fresh at each experiment) Ingredients: - Hypothermosol - Protease from Bacillus Licheniformis (100 mg/mL stock solution in PBS) - EDTA 10 mM For 1 mL of dissociation mix add: - 850 microlitres of Hypothermosol - 100 microlitres of protease (Final concentration:10 mg/mL) - 50 microlitres of EDTA (Final concentration: 0.5 mM) Undetermined
Shake the brush into the buffer to put cells in suspension then spin the tube for 2 min at 150g to remove residual cells from the brush.

Incubate cells on ice for 30 min, with gentle trituration with needles 5 times every 5 min. Use needle with decreasing sizes from 21G to 23G. If storage and/or transportation has been performed for at least 60 min, skip the incubation step, only perform trituration steps. 00:30:00, Incubation 00:05:00, Trituration Undetermined
Discard the cytology brush and observe cells under an inverted microscope. Quick-Read 10 Chamber Slide Globe Scientific Catalog #3805
Inactivate protease by adding 200 µL of Inactivation buffer (HBSS containing 2% BSA) undetermined, Inactivation buffer Prepare Inactivation buffer : HBSS : 40 mL 10% BSA stock: 10 mL
Spin at 400g for 5 min at 4°C
Discard supernatant leaving 10 µL of residual liquid on the pellet.

Observe cells under an inverted microscope to evaluate red blood cells (RBC) content. RBC content is better evaluated using an automated cell counter such as Countess, after addition of Hoechst 33342 to an aliquot of the cell suspension to discriminate nucleated cells from non-nucleated cells.

If RBC content iis lower than 50%, go directly to step 18. Perform RBC lysis: add 1.8 mL of Ammonium Chloride 0.8% to 200 µL of cell suspension (9 volumes), transfer in a 5 mL eppendorf. Ammonium Chloride Solution 100 mL STEMCELL Technologies Inc. Catalog #7800 undetermined, Ammonium Chloride 9 vol. for 1 cell vol.
Incubate on ice for 10 min. 00:10:00, RBC lysis Undetermined
Add 400 µL of Inactivation buffer undetermined, Inactivation buffer
Spin at 400g for 5 min at 4°C
Resuspend in 200 µL of wash buffer (HBSS + 1% BSA) Prepare Wash buffer : HBSS : 20 mL HBSS/BSA 2%: 20 mL
Spin at 400g for 5 min at 4°C
Resuspend in 250 µL of wash buffer and monitor correct RBC lysis undetermined, wash buffer
Discard supernatant leaving 10 µL of residual liquid on the pellet.
Add 1 mL of wash buffer undetermined, wash buffer
Discard supernatant leaving 10 µL of residual liquid on the pellet.
Resuspend in 500 µL of wash buffer undetermined, wash buffer
Discard supernatant leaving 10 µL of residual liquid on the pellet.
Resuspend in 50 µL of resuspension buffer (HBSS + 0.05% BSA). 50 µL, Resuspension buffer Prepare Resuspension buffer : HBSS : 39 mL HBSS/BSA 2%: 1 mL

Mix 10 µL of cells with 10 µL of cell counting solution (HBSS with 20 µg/mL Hoechst 33342 and NucGreen). Incubate for 1 min at room temperature. Hoechst 33342, Trihydrochloride, Trihydrate - 10 mg/mL Solution in Water Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #H3570 NucGreen™ Dead 488 ReadyProbes™ Reagent Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #R37109 Preparation of cell staining reagent: - HBSS: 500 µL - Hoechst 33342 (10 mg/mL): 1 µL - NucGreen™ Dead 488 ReadyProbes™ Reagent: 1 drop 00:01:00
Filter cell suspension through Flowmi cell strainer Flowmi cell strainer Contributed by users Catalog #H13680-0040 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=taS1BuTnvds
Add 500 µL of wash buffer to filtered cells. undetermined, wash buffer

Count with Countess automated cell counter using both sides of chambers. Monitor the percentage of nucleated cells (Hoechst +) and dead cells (GFP+). (Thermo Fisher Scientific, AMQAF1000, Countess™ II FL Automated Cell Counter with Dapi and GFP cubes) Countess report after NucGreen and Hoescht33342 staining Countess bright field image after NucGreen and Hoescht33342 staining Countess Dapi image after NucGreen and Hoescht33342 staining Floid microscope image after NucGreen and Hoescht33342 staining

If cells are processed directly after brushing, use DPBS instead of hypothermosol and go directly to step 4. If transportation or storage is necessary: place tube on ice or in polystyrene box containing ice packs. Brushings can be stored for 60 min in dissociation buffer.
Adjust concentration to a range of 700 to 1000 cells/µL (with wash buffer) for 10X Chromium. Monitor final cell concentration.
creator: Laure-Emmanuelle Zaragosi
